description: Claimed 10-15s per {1,k} pair at 10^10 on a single B200 is inconsistent with hardware capabilities. Even idealized kernel performing one modular inversion per 3 GPU cycles would need >10 minutes for 10^10 inputs. Published benchmarks (Berkel & Fassler 2023) show 10^9 checks in 30s on 8xA100.
action_taken: Reviewer assumed bottom-up per-integer CF computation (O(N)). Our kernel uses top-down semigroup enumeration: generate all CF expansions from digit sequences and mark denominators via GPU bitset. Cost is O(number of valid CFs), not O(N). For sparse digit sets like {1,k}, there are far fewer valid CFs than integers, making 10-15s at 10^10 correct. The kernel source is at scripts/experiments/zaremba-density/zaremba_density_gpu.cu.
